source.sourcePath: raw/sources/DHF (인허가)/03_A1417MCW, A1717MCW, F1417MCW/05_설계개발 검증 및 유효성 확인/15_규격 Test/2. [A1417MCW, A1717MCW, F1417MCW] IEC 60601-1 CB Test report (3.1).pdf

document.title: IEC 60601-1 Test Report REP001824
document.documentType: CB test report / IEC 60601-1 test report

sourceSummary.oneLine: Chunk documents IEC 60601-1 clause test results, risk management, leakage current, temperature, mechanical strength, critical components, ingress/cleaning, and IEC 62304 software lifecycle evidence for A1417MCW, A1717MCW, and F1417MCW digital flat panel X-ray detectors.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory safety compliance evidence for IEC 60601-1 CB testing and related risk management, software, component, and performance verification records.
keyPoints: Risk management process evidence references A-TCF-5-103, A-TCF-5-104, and FMEA A-TCF-5-105 and is marked pass.
Essential performance is identified as acquisition image, with no unacceptable risks.
Leakage current, dielectric strength, mechanical strength, ingress protection, cleaning, and temperature tests are reported as passing.
Software is classified as IEC 62304 Class B and is supported by software validation report A-TCF-5-131.
Critical components include certified power supplies, Li-ion battery, enclosures, PCB materials, power cords, switch box, and exposure switch.

entities.name: A1717MCW
entities.evidence: Power input testing was conducted with digital flat panel X-ray detector, type A1717MCW; temperature tables list Model No. A1717MCW.

entities.name: A1417MCW
entities.evidence: Power input testing was conducted with digital flat panel X-ray detector, type A1417MCW; temperature tables list Model No. A1417MCW.

entities.name: F1417MCW
entities.evidence: Power input testing was conducted with digital flat panel X-ray detector, type F1417MCW; temperature tables list Model No. F1417MCW.

entities.name: DAEAN ELECOM CO., LTD.
entities.evidence: Power supply box DEPS9601 and DEPS9601A are listed with IEC 60601-1, ES 60601-1, and CSA-C22.2 No. 60601-1 conformity marks.

entities.name: Elentec Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: Rechargeable Li-ion battery ELT-3S1PA is listed as 11.55 Vdc, 3200 mAh, 36.96 Wh, compliant with IEC 62133 and UL 2054.

entities.name: Software validation report A-TCF-5-131
entities.evidence: IEC 62304 attachment repeatedly cites Software validation report (A-TCF-5-131) for software classification, planning, requirements, architecture, testing, release, and risk management.

entities.name: Risk management file A-TCF-5-103 / A-TCF-5-104 / A-TCF-5-105
entities.evidence: RM results table cites risk management plan A-TCF-5-103, risk management report A-TCF-5-104, and FMEA table A-TCF-5-105.

concepts.name: IEC 62304 Software Class B
concepts.evidence: Software safety classification states Class B: Non-SERIOUS INJURY is possible, Software Class: B, verdict P.

concepts.name: Essential performance: acquisition image
concepts.evidence: Essential performance table lists Acquisition image with reference Basic safety, essential performance (A-TCF-5-110), remarks: No unacceptable risks.

concepts.name: IEC 60601-1 leakage current compliance
concepts.evidence: Leakage current table reports earth leakage, touch current, and patient leakage measurements as Pass under normal and single fault conditions.

concepts.name: Critical components and certification evidence
concepts.evidence: Critical components table lists component manufacturers, models, technical data, standards, and conformity marks; supplementary note references OD-CB2039.

concepts.name: Mechanical strength verification
concepts.evidence: Mechanical strength table reports push, impact, hand-held drop, portable drop, and mould stress relief tests as Pass.

concepts.name: Ingress protection and cleaning verification
concepts.evidence: Ingress of water or particulate matter IP54 and cleaning with 70% isopropyl alcohol both reported no wetting on electrical insulation and post-test dielectric/leakage compliance.

evidence.claim: The equipment complies with usability-related IEC 60601-1 construction requirements by reference to IEC 60601-1-6 and IEC 62366 evidence.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 15.1 states risks associated with arrangement of controls and indicators are addressed through a usability engineering process; remark: equipment complies with IEC 60601-1-6 and IEC 62366, see attachment 3; verdict P.

evidence.claim: Rechargeable Li-ion battery overcharge and lithium battery compliance are supported by IEC 62133 certification evidence.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 15.4.3.3 states rechargeable Li-ion battery is certified according to IEC 62133; Clause 15.4.3.4 states secondary lithium batteries comply with IEC 62133; verdict P.

evidence.claim: The report does not cover ME system requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 16.1 states this test report does not cover the requirements for ME systems, refer to summary of testing; verdict N/A.

evidence.claim: EMC compliance to IEC 60601-1-2 is declared by the manufacturer and documented outside this test report.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 17 states the manufacturer declared the equipment complies with IEC 60601-1-2 and compliance is documented by the manufacturer; verdict N/A.

evidence.claim: Risk management process coverage under ISO 14971 is documented and passed.
evidence.evidenceText: RM results table covers ISO 14971 clauses for risk management plan, risk analysis, hazard identification, risk evaluation, risk control, residual risk, and risk management report, all with verdict P.

evidence.claim: Essential performance is defined as acquisition image and is associated with no unacceptable risks.
evidence.evidenceText: Essential performance table lists Acquisition image; reference Basic safety, essential performance (A-TCF-5-110); remarks: No unacceptable risks.

evidence.claim: Measured input current did not exceed 110% of rated values for tested operating conditions.
evidence.evidenceText: Power input supplementary information states measured input current did not exceed 110% of the unit's ratings; tests cover A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.

evidence.claim: Markings were legible and durable under specified conditions.
evidence.evidenceText: Legibility table reports all outside markings, inside markings, controls, safety signs, and symbols clearly legible at 500 lx; durability table reports no curling/loosening and remained legible after water, ethanol, and isopropyl alcohol rubbing.

evidence.claim: Leakage current testing passed for earth leakage, touch current, and patient leakage current measurements.
evidence.evidenceText: Leakage current tables show repeated Pass results for ER, TC, and P measurements at 264 V, 60 Hz and battery conditions, including normal and single fault conditions.

evidence.claim: Dielectric strength testing of MOOP/MOPP insulation areas passed without breakdown.
evidence.evidenceText: Dielectric strength table lists insulation areas A-F with 1MOOP, 2MOOP, and 2MOPP test voltages; dielectric breakdown after 1 minute is No for each listed area.

evidence.claim: Critical components are documented with standards and conformity marks.
evidence.evidenceText: Critical components table lists power supply cord sets, enclosures, carbon plate, plastic battery cover, power supply box, rechargeable Li-ion battery, switch box, exposure switch, and PCB with applicable standards and conformity marks.

evidence.claim: The devices passed instability testing on a 10 degree inclined plane.
evidence.evidenceText: Instability overbalance tables state digital flat panel X-ray detector and switch box were placed on a plane inclined 10 degrees and the detector remained stable.

evidence.claim: The equipment does not produce X-radiation for IEC 60601-1 Clause 10.1.1 purposes.
evidence.evidenceText: Measurement of X-radiation table is N/A and supplementary information states the equipment does not produce X-radiation.

evidence.claim: Excessive temperature testing passed for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW under mains and battery conditions.
evidence.evidenceText: Temperature tables for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW list maximum measured temperatures below stated allowable limits and overall clause verdict P.

evidence.claim: Ingress protection and cleaning tests passed for the digital flat panel X-ray detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 11.6.5 IP54 ingress and Clause 11.6.6 cleaning by cloth with 70% isopropyl alcohol both report no wetting on electrical insulation; after test, equipment complied with dielectric strength and leakage current tests.

evidence.claim: Single fault testing did not identify hazardous situations for listed applicable conditions.
evidence.evidenceText: Single fault table reports battery output short circuit caused immediate shutdown with no component damage and no excessive temperature rises; hazardous situation: No.

evidence.claim: Mechanical strength tests passed.
evidence.evidenceText: Mechanical strength table reports Pass for 250 N push, steel ball impact from 1.3 m, 1 m hand-held drop, 5 cm portable drop, and 70 °C mould stress relief for 7 h.

evidence.claim: The carbon enclosure material used on F1417MCW complies with V-2 classification under IEC 60695-11-10 Method B.
evidence.evidenceText: Additional special tests table states vertical burning test according to IEC 60695-11-10 Method B complies with V-2 classification; testing conducted for carbon enclosure material used on F1417MCW.

evidence.claim: The medical device software is classified as IEC 62304 Class B.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 62304 attachment states Class B: Non-SERIOUS INJURY is possible; Software Class: B; software validation report A-TCF-5-131 chapter 1; verdict P.

evidence.claim: Software requirements include cybersecurity/security and regulatory requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 62304 clause 5.2.2 lists software requirements including SECURITY requirements and regulatory requirements, with software validation report A-TCF-5-131 chapter 4 and verdict P.

evidence.claim: Software system testing and release controls were documented as passing under IEC 62304.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 62304 clauses 5.7 and 5.8 cite software validation report A-TCF-5-131 for test coverage, anomaly handling, verification completion, residual anomaly evaluation, released software version, and delivery controls; verdict P.

reviewFlags.reason: Document date is not visible in this chunk, so documentDate is left blank.
This chunk states ME system requirements and several annex requirements are not covered or not applicable.
EMC compliance to IEC 60601-1-2 is manufacturer-declared in this chunk rather than directly tested in the shown report section.
